name.zh: 阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬

history.en: Developed in the Austrian Alps during the mid-19th century as a durable scent hound for tracking wounded deer, boar, and hare over rugged terrain. Descended from larger Austrian hounds crossed with Dachshunds, it was a favorite of Crown Prince Rudolf of Habsburg and is officially recognized by the FCI.
history.zh: 19世纪中期在奥地利阿尔卑斯地区培育而成，是一种耐用的嗅觉猎犬，用于在崎岖地形追踪受伤的鹿、野猪和野兔。由大型奥地利猎犬与腊肠犬杂交而来，曾是哈布斯堡王朝鲁道夫王储的最爱，并获FCI正式认可。

overview.en: The Alpine Dachsbracke is a medium-sized, robust scent hound originally developed in the Austrian Alps for tracking wounded game over challenging terrain. This breed is intelligent, courageous, and deeply loyal to its family, but also highly work-focused and energetic. They possess a keen nose and a determined, persistent nature, making them exceptional tracking dogs. Despite their hunting drive, they are affectionate and gentle at home, forming strong bonds with their owners. They are generally reserved with strangers but not aggressive. Their personality combines the independence of a hound with the eagerness to please their handler, requiring a confident and consistent owner. They are not suited for a sedentary lifestyle and thrive when given a purpose, whether in hunting, scent work, or active outdoor adventures. Their courage and stamina are remarkable, and they are known for their steady temperament in the field.
overview.zh: 阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬是一种中型、结实的嗅觉猎犬，最初在奥地利阿尔卑斯山脉培育，用于在崎岖地形追踪受伤的猎物。该品种聪明、勇敢，对家庭极其忠诚，同时高度专注于工作且精力充沛。它们拥有敏锐的嗅觉和坚定、执着的天性，是卓越的追踪犬。尽管有狩猎驱动力，它们在家庭环境中充满感情、性格温和，与主人建立深厚纽带。它们通常对陌生人保持矜持，但不具攻击性。其性格融合了猎犬的独立性和取悦驯导者的意愿，需要自信且前后一致的主人。它们不适合久坐不动的生活方式，在有任务时（无论是狩猎、嗅闻工作还是活跃的户外探险）才能茁壮成长。它们的勇气和耐力令人瞩目，并在野外以其稳定的性情而闻名。

care.en: Daily care for the Alpine Dachsbracke is straightforward but requires commitment. Their short, dense double coat sheds moderately year-round and more heavily seasonally; brushing twice a week with a rubber curry brush or hound glove removes loose hair and keeps the coat healthy. Bathing is only needed when dirty. Regular ear checks and cleaning are essential to prevent infections, especially after outdoor activities. Nail trimming every few weeks and dental care (brushing or dental chews) should be part of the routine. Exercise is critical: they need at least 60–90 minutes of vigorous activity daily, including long walks, runs, or off-leash exploration in safe areas. Mental stimulation through scent games or tracking exercises is equally important. Annual veterinary check-ups, vaccinations, and parasite prevention are mandatory. They are generally healthy but can be prone to hip dysplasia and ear infections, so weight management and ear hygiene are key.
care.zh: 阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬的日常护理简单但需要坚持。它们短而密的双层被毛全年适度脱落，换季时更明显；每周用橡胶马鬃刷或猎犬手套梳理两次，可去除死毛并保持被毛健康。仅在脏污时需要洗澡。定期检查和清洁耳朵至关重要，尤其是在户外活动后，以防感染。每隔几周修剪指甲，并坚持牙齿护理（刷牙或提供洁齿骨）。运动是关键：它们每天至少需要60至90分钟的剧烈活动，包括长途散步、跑步或在安全区域自由探索。通过嗅闻游戏或追踪训练进行的精神刺激同样重要。每年一次兽医体检、疫苗接种和寄生虫预防必不可少。它们通常健康，但可能易患髋关节发育不良和耳部感染，因此体重管理和耳朵卫生是重点。

training.en: Training an Alpine Dachsbracke requires patience, consistency, and positive reinforcement. As a scent hound, they can be independent and easily distracted by interesting smells, so early socialization and obedience training are crucial. Start with basic commands and gradually introduce more challenging tasks. They respond best to reward-based methods using treats, praise, or play, as harsh corrections can damage their trust. Their strong prey drive means recall training in a controlled environment is essential before off-leash freedom. Scent work and tracking games tap into their natural instincts and provide excellent mental stimulation. Keep sessions short and engaging to hold their interest. They are intelligent but can be stubborn, so a firm but gentle hand works best. Consistent leadership and clear boundaries help them feel secure. They excel in hunting, nose work, and canine sports like rally or obedience, making training a rewarding experience for both dog and owner.
training.zh: 训练阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬需要耐心、一致性和正向强化。作为嗅觉猎犬，它们可能独立且容易被有趣的气味分心，因此早期社会化及服从训练至关重要。从基础命令开始，逐步引入更具挑战性的任务。它们对基于奖励的方法（使用零食、表扬或游戏）反应最佳，严厉的纠正会损害它们的信任。它们强烈的猎物驱动力意味着在允许自由活动之前，必须在受控环境中完成召回训练。嗅闻工作和追踪游戏可激发其天性，并提供极佳的精神刺激。保持训练时间短而有趣以维持其兴趣。它们聪明但可能固执，因此坚定而温和的手腕最为有效。一致的领导和清晰的界限能帮助它们感到安全。它们在狩猎、嗅闻工作以及如敏捷或服从等犬类运动中表现出色，使训练成为狗与主人都获益的体验。

diet.en: The Alpine Dachsbracke requires a balanced, high-quality diet to support its active lifestyle. A protein-rich food (from sources like chicken, lamb, or fish) is ideal for muscle maintenance, along with healthy fats for energy and coat health. Carbohydrates from whole grains or vegetables provide sustained fuel. Divide daily food into two meals to reduce the risk of bloat (gastric dilatation-volvulus), which can affect deep-chested breeds. Portion sizes depend on age, weight, and activity level; typically, an adult dog needs about 1.5 to 2.5 cups of premium dry food per day. Avoid overfeeding, as this breed can gain weight easily, which stresses joints. Fresh water should always be available. Treats should be limited and used primarily for training. Consider joint supplements (glucosamine and chondroitin) for older dogs or those prone to hip issues. Always consult a veterinarian for personalized feeding plans, especially for puppies or working dogs with higher energy demands.
diet.zh: 阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬需要均衡、高质量的食物以支持其活跃的生活方式。富含蛋白质的食物（来自鸡肉、羊肉或鱼类等来源）是维持肌肉的理想选择，搭配健康脂肪以提供能量和被毛健康。来自全谷物或蔬菜的碳水化合物提供持续的能量。将每日食物分为两餐，以降低胃扩张-扭转的风险（该风险可能影响胸部较深的品种）。份量取决于年龄、体重和活动水平；通常，成年犬每天约需1.5至2.5杯优质干粮。避免过量喂食，因为该品种容易发胖，从而给关节带来压力。应始终提供新鲜饮用水。零食应限制使用，主要用于训练。对于年长犬或易患髋关节问题的犬，可考虑关节补充剂（葡萄糖胺和软骨素）。请务必咨询兽医以制定个性化喂养计划，尤其是对于能量需求更高的幼犬或工作犬。

livingTips.en: The Alpine Dachsbracke is best suited for a home with a securely fenced yard, as they love to explore and follow scents. Apartment living is possible only if they receive extensive daily exercise and mental stimulation, but it is not ideal due to their energy and potential baying. They thrive in rural or suburban settings with access to nature. They are generally good with children, especially when raised with them, but supervision is recommended due to their boisterous play style. Their hound nature means they may chase small pets like cats or rabbits unless socialized early, but they can coexist peacefully with other dogs. They are not excessive barkers indoors but will vocalize when tracking or excited. They need a comfortable, quiet space to rest after activity. This breed does not tolerate boredom well and may become destructive if under-exercised. Overall, they are loyal family companions for active owners who can provide structure, exercise, and purpose.
livingTips.zh: 阿尔卑斯达克斯布拉克犬最适合拥有安全围栏庭院的家庭，因为它们喜欢探索和追随气味。公寓生活仅在能提供大量日常运动和精神刺激的情况下才可能，但由于其精力旺盛和可能吠叫，并非理想选择。它们在能接触自然的乡村或郊区环境中茁壮成长。它们通常对孩子友善，尤其是一起长大的情况下，但由于其喧闹的玩耍方式，建议进行监督。其猎犬天性意味着它们可能会追逐猫或兔子等小宠物，除非早期进行社会化，但它们可以与其他狗和平共处。它们在室内不会过度吠叫，但在追踪或兴奋时会发声。它们需要一个舒适安静的空间在活动后休息。该品种不耐无聊，如果运动不足可能会变得具有破坏性。总之，对于能够提供结构、运动和使命感的活跃主人来说，它们是忠诚的家庭伴侣。
